title: Change date-of-birth mid-call

facts.reason: needs a follow-up after an ER visit last week

objective: Book a follow-up appointment. State your birth year as 1983 confidently and let the agent record it — do NOT correct it right away. Only several turns later, after you've given other details and the agent has moved on, casually realize the mistake ('oh wait, I think I gave you the wrong birth year earlier') and correct it to 1982. The point is to see whether the agent updates the stale value it already saved.
probes: Does the agent use the corrected DOB or the stale first value?
Does the agent even notice or acknowledge the correction?
